pubmed_links: [Siekierska et al. (2022) Modulating effects of FGF12 variants on NaV1.2 and NaV1.6 associated with developmental and epileptic encephalopathy and autism spectrum disorder](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36029553/); [Pippucci et al. (2020) Epilepsy phenotype in individuals with chromosomal duplication encompassing FGF12](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32431746/); [Guella et al. (2016) Phenytoin-responsive epileptic encephalopathy with a tandem duplication involving FGF12](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27386478/)
summary: Variants in FGF12, encoding a fibroblast homologous factor that modulates voltage-gated sodium channels NaV1.2 and NaV1.6, cause developmental and epileptic encephalopathy-47 and have been associated with autism spectrum disorder; the gene plays a critical role in neuronal excitability, differentiation, and maturation. (emerging evidence)
